name: (+)-inotropic
description: An agent that alters the force or energy of muscular contractions. Positively inotropic agents increase the strength of muscular contraction.

name: (-)-chronotropic
description: An agent that may change the heart rate by affecting the nerves controlling the heart, or by changing the rhythm produced by the sinoatrial node. Negative chronotropes decrease heart rate.

name: (-)-inotropic
description: An agent that alters the force or energy of muscular contractions. Negatively inotropic agents weaken the force of muscular contractions.

name: absorbent
description: A substance or item able to soak liquid easily.

name: acantholytic
description: Causing loss of intercellular connections, such as desmosomes, resulting in loss of cohesion between keratinocytes.

name: adaptogenic
description: A substance or compounds that results in stabilization of physiological processes and promotion of homeostasis, an example being by decreased cellular sensitivity to stress.

name: addictive
description: A substance causing dependency, in spite of effects.

name: aggregant
description: An agent that promotes conjugation or collection of particulars into a whole.

name: allelochemic
description: Chemicals, released from plants, that cause an interaction between plants and other living organisms.

name: allelopathic
description: Biochemicals produced by an organism that influence the growth, survival, and reproduction of other organisms.

name: amblyopic
description: A toxic sunstance causing amblyopia by damaging the optic nerve, such as lead, methanol, antifreeze, or certain drugs.

name: amphiglycemic
description: An agent that stabilizes blood sugar levels.

name: anaphrodisiac
description: An agent that quells or blunts the libido.

name: anaphylactic
description: Allergent causing anaphylaxis, an allergic reaction with symptoms such as itchy rash, throat swelling, low blood pressure.

name: ancylostomicide
description: An agent destructive to Ancylostoma genus of nematodes.

name: androgenic
description: An agent, usually a steroid hormone, that stimulates or controls the development and maintenance of male characteristics in vertebrates by binding to androgen receptors. This includes the activity of the accessory male sex organs and development of male secondary sex characteristics.

name: anemiagenic
description: An agent causing anemia. For example, arsenic can cause hemolytic anemia.

name: anesthetic
description: A drug that causes anesthesia, a reversible loss of sensation.

name: anhydrotic
description: An agent that suppresses sweating. Denoting a reduction or absence of sweat glands, characteristic of congenital ectodermal defect and anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia.

name: anti acid
description: A substance that neutralizes acidity, such as stomach acidity that causes heartburn.

name: anti acromegalic
description: An agent that suppresses the over production of growth hormone related to acromegaly.

name: anti ADD
description: A drug used to treat attention deficit disorder, often a stimulant that promotes concentration and attention.

name: anti Addisonian
description: An agent, often a steroid, used to manage addison's disease characterized by underactivity of the adrenal glands.

name: anti adhesive
description: An agent that prevents adhesion, or the clinging together of dissimilar particles or surfaces.

name: anti aggregant
description: An agent that prevents the conjugation or collection of particulars into a whole.

name: anti alkali
description: An agent that reduces the pH of a solution.

name: anti alkalotic
description: An agent that relieves excessively alkiline conditions of body fluids or tissues that may cause weakness or cramps.

name: anti amyloid-beta
description: An agent, such as an antibody, that lowers amyloid levels in an an Alzheimer affected brain.

name: anti apoptotic
description: An agent that inhibits the process of apoptosis, or programmed cell death.

name: anti asphyctic
description: An agent that can enhance the blood's ability to carry oxygen.

name: anti ataxic
description: A drug used to treat ataxia, a stimulant of movement coordination centres, such as the cerebellum.

name: anti benign prostatic hyperplasia
description: A drug used to aleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia such as urinary problems.

name: anti bradykinetic
description: A drug used to stimulate motor function, for patients suffering from slowness of movement such as in the case of Parkinson's.

name: anti burn
description: An agent that is used in the treatment of burn wounds.

name: anti calculic
description: An agent that acts againt gall bladder stones.

name: anti capillary-fragility
description: An agent or drug that strengthens capillay walls to prevent rupture.

name: anti carpal-tunnel
description: A drug used to alleviate the sympoms of carpal-tunnel syndrome (inflammation, pain, etc), such as a shot of cortisone in the wrist.

name: anti CFS
description: An agent used to alleviate some symptoms of chronic fatigue syndrome, such as a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory agent in treatment of myalgias and arthralgias.

name: anti cheilitic
description: An agent used to treat cheilitis. Corticosteroids and anti-inflammatory agents ar examples.

name: anti cheilotic
description: An agent used to treat the cheilosis skin condition. Antifungal drugs containing hydrocortisone are an example.

name: anti chilblain
description: A drug that stimulates dilation of blood vessels, used in the tretment of chilblains.

name: anti cholecystosic
description: An agent that relieves symptoms, such as inflammation, for conditions affecting the gallbladder in which there are chronic inflammatory changes with hyperplasia of all the tissue elements.

name: anti choleretic
description: A drug that inhibits overproduction of bile in the liver.

name: anti cholestatic
description: A drug that stimulates the output of bile from the liver.

name: anti cholesteremic
description: An agent that promotes the reduction of cholesterol levels in the blood.

name: anti cholesterogenic
description: An agent that promotes the reduction of total circulating cholesterol.

name: anti clastogen
description: A drug acting to inhibit mutagenic agents that disrupt or break chromosomes.

name: anti climacteric
description: A drug used to relieve effects of the male menopause, such as diminished sexual activity.

name: anti coccidioid
description: A drug used as a preventative measure for coccidiosis in chicken, to minimizes the number of infective oocytes of the Emeria species that buid up in the environment.

name: anti collagenic
description: An agent, or antibody, that binds collagen and prevents buildup in connective tissues. It may be used to treat heart disease.

name: anti colonospasmic
description: An agent that relieves effects of colonospasm, a disorder marked by decreased motility of the intestine.

name: anti complement
description: A substance that combines with a complement component and neutralizes its action by preventing its union with anantibody.

name: anti complementary
description: A substance possessing the power to diminish or abolish the action of a complement.

name: anti corneitic
description: An agent that diminishes inflammation of the cornea.

name: anti corpus-luteum
description: An agent, or antibody, used to detect ovarian disfunction based on corpus luteum hormone levels.

name: anti C-reactive-protein
description: An antobody agains C-reactive protein used as a marker of inflammation. There is a link between high production of C-reactive protein and risk of developing cancer.

name: anti cryptococcic
description: An antibody that may be used to detect cryptococcic meningitis.

name: anti CTS
description: An antibody against a cathepsin, proteases involved in intracellular degradation and turnover of proteins. These may be linked to tumor invasion and metastasis.

name: anti CVI
description: An antibiotic acting against colicin, a protein involved in E. coli immunity.

name: anti cystinuric
description: An agent that diminishes the formation of cysteine stones in the kydneys, ureter, or bladder.

name: anti cystitic
description: An agent that diminishes inflammation of the urinary bladder.

name: anti dandruff
description: An agent that reduces the production or flaking of dandruff.

name: anti decubitic
description: An agent, topical drug, or nutritional factor used to treat or prevent bed sores.

name: anti dermatophytic
description: An agent, or essential oil, used to treat fungal infections of skin, hair, or nails.

name: anti dipsomanic
description: An agent that may reduce the compulsion to drink alcoholic beverages.

name: anti discitic
description: An agent that may reduce inflammation of an intervertebral disc or disc space, often related to infections.

name: anti discotic
description: An agent that prevents disc-shaped liquid crystalization.

name: anti-DNA
description: A DNA binding antibody used for diagnostic purposes.

name: anti drepanocytic
description: An agent that may prevent or combat the sickling of erythrocytes characteristic of sickle cell anemia.

name: anti dysgeuzic
description: An agent that may be used to relieve the distortion of taste characteristic of dysgeusia.

name: anti dyskinetic
description: An agent that relieves or prevents dyskinesia, which is impairment of the power of voluntary movement.

name: anti ecchymotic
description: An agent that prevents ecchymosis, or the rupture of blood vessels that leads to the passage of blood into subcutaneous tissue.

name: anti edemic
description: An agent that relieves or prevents the abnormal accumulation of fluid in the circulatory system or tissues, a condition known as edema.

name: anti embolic
description: An agent that desintegrates a blood clot, or foreign material in the blood.

name: anti endometriotic
description: An agent or drug that relieves endometriosis, a condition resulting from the appearance of endometrial tissue outside the uterus and causing pelvic pain.

name: anti enteritic
description: An anti-inflammtory agent that relieves swelling of the intestine, or enteritis.

name: anti enterococcic
description: An antibody binding to Enterococcus specific targets. The Enterococcus genus are lactic acid bacteria.

name: anti enuretic
description: An agent that prevents discharge of urine. Involuntary discharge of urine is a condition known as enuresis.

name: anti enzymatic
description: An agent or substance that inhibits or counteracts the action of an enzyme.

name: anti episiotomic
name_zh: 抗會陰切開術（促進會陰癒合）
description: An agent, such as an anti-inflammatory drug, used to treat an episiotomic tear after childbirth.

name: anti escherichic
description: An antibody binding to Escherichia specific targets.

name: anti esophagitic
description: An anti-inflammatory agent used to treat inflammation of the esophagus.

name: anti exudative
description: An agent that relieves the oozing of fluid or other materials from cells and tissues, which may be a result of inflammation or injury.

name: anti feedant
description: Any substance that inhibits normal feeding behaviour. A naturally occurring substance in certain plants that adversely affects insects or other animals that eat them.

name: anti fertility
description: An agent or substance that inhibits the ability to produce offspring. A contraceptive.

name: anti fibrinolytic
description: Lysine-like drugs that interfere with the formation of the fibrinolytic enzyme plasmin from its precursor plasminogen by plasminogen activators which takes place mainly in lysine rich areas on the surface of fibrin. These drugs block the binding sites of the enzymes or plasminogen respectively and thus stop plasmin formation.

name: anti fibrosarcomic
description: An agent or drug that acts to stunt proliferation of sarcoma in which the predominant cell type is a malignant fibroblast.

name: anti fibrositic
description: An agent, such as an anti-inflammatory agent, that relieves inflammation, degeneration, or metabolic derangement of connective tissue structures (especially joints and related structures).

name: anti fibrotic
description: An agent that stunts excessive formation of fibrous connective tissue in an organ or tissue during a reparative or reactive process.

name: anti filarial
description: An agent destructive to filariae, which cause lymphatic filariasis.

name: anti furuncular
description: An agent that relieves skin sores.

name: anti galactagogue
description: An agent, such as a herbal extract, that prevents or decreases the secretion of milk in nursing mothers.

name: anti gallstone
description: An agent, dietary or medical, that prevents the formation of gallstones.

name: anti gastric
description: An antibody to gastric parietal cells, associated with auitoimmune gastritis and pernicious anaemia.

name: anti gastritic
description: An anti-inflammatory agent that relieves inflammation of the stomach lining.

name: antigenic
description: A substance that when introduced into the body stimulates the production of an antibody. Antigens include toxins,bacteria, foreign blood cells, and the cells of transplanted organs.

name: anti giardial
description: An agent or drug used to treate instestinal infection caused by Giardia intestinalis.

name: anti glaucomic
description: An agent, nutritional or medical, used to treat damage of the optic nerve and vision loss.

name: anti gliomic
description: An agent or drug that acts to stunt proliferation of glioma, a malignant tumor of the glial tissue of the nervous system.

name: anti glossitic
description: An agent that relieves inflammation of the tongue, or glossitis.

name: anti glutamaergic
description: An agent or drug that modulates the transmission of glutamate, or inhibits glutamate currents, for muscle relaxation, antiseizure, or neuroprotective effects. May be used for ALS.

name: anti glycosuric
description: An agent that prevents the excess accumulation of sugar in urine, as in diabetes.

name: anti gonadotropic
description: An agent or drug which suppresses the activity and/or downstream effects of one or both of the gonadotropins, follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and luteinizing hormone (LH). Used in treatment of hormonally-sensitive cancers, to delay precocious puberty and puberty in transgender youth, as a form of chemical castration to reduce the sex drives of individuals (namely males) with hypersexuality or pedophilia, and to treat estrogen-associated conditions in women such as menorrhagia and endometriosis.

name: anti grey
description: An agent that prevents hair whitening.

name: anti glucose-tolerance-factor
description: An antibody binding glucose tolerence factor (GTF), which is a dietary agent with high insulin mimetic and insulin potentiating activity.

name: anti halitosic
description: An agent that relieves bad breath.

name: anti heartburn
description: An agent that relieves the effects caused by acid regurgitation into the esophagus.

name: anti hematuric
description: An agent that prevents the release of blood into urine.

name: anti hiccup
description: An agent that relieves hiccups, or the involuntary contraction of the diaphragm.

name: anti histoplasmotic
description: An anti-fungal agent used to treat infections caused by breathing in spores of a fungus often found in bird and bat droppings.

name: anti homocystinuric
description: An antibody used to detect  abnormal accumulation of homocysteine and its metabolites in blood and urine, a condition known as homocystinuria.

name: anti hot-flash
description: An agent or hormone, such as estrogen, used to treat hot flashes, or sudden feelings of feverish heat, typically as a symptom of menopause.

name: anti hydrocoele
description: An agent that relieves abnormal accumulation of fluid in the scrotum, within a thin membrane adjacent to the testicle called the processus or tunica vaginalis.

name: anti hydrophobic
description: An agent that prevents the aggregation of hydrophobic substances in aqueous solution.

name: anti hyperactivity
description: An agent or drug, which may be a stimulant, used to treat ADHD.

name: anti hyperammonemic
description: An agent that lowers or controls ammonia levels in the blood.

name: anti hypercholesterolemic
description: An agent that prevents or controls an increase of cholesterol in the blood.

name: anti hyperglycemic
description: An agent that lowers elevated blood glucose levels.

name: anti hyperinsulinemic
description: An agent that lowers elevated blood insulin levels.

name: anti hyperkeratotic
description: An agent that relieves severe flaking and cracking of the skin.

name: anti hyperkinetic
description: An agent or drug that reduces muscular activity, as a result of excessive abnormal movements, excessive normal movements, or a combination of both.

name: anti hyperleptinemic
description: An agent that reduces the levels of leptins in the bloodstream.

name: anti hypogonadic
description: An agent that may boost sex hormone biosynthesis of under active gonads.

name: anti hypoxic
description: An agent that acts against hypoxic cells, such as by sensitizing hypoxic tumor cells to the effects of radiation.

name: anti ichthyosic
description: An agent that prevents or relieves severly dry, scaly skin caused by ichthyosis.

name: anti ileus
description: An agent used to treat an obstruction of the ileum or other part of the intestine.

name: anti implantation
description: An agent that prevents the adherence of the blastocyst to the wall of the uterus.

name: anti insulinogenic
description: An agent that inhibits or controls the production of insulin.

name: anti iritic
description: An agent, such as an anti-inflammatory agent, that relieves inflammation of the iris of the eye.

name: anti keratotic
description: An agent acting against keratosis, overgrowth of horny tissue of the skin (such as warts).

name: anti klebsiellic
description: An antibody that binds Klebsiella specific target. Klebsiella is  a genus of nonmotile, Gram-negative, oxidase-negative, rod-shaped bacteria with a prominent polysaccharide-based capsule.

name: anti lactagogue
description: An agent that decreases the flow of breast milk in a woman.

name: anti leucodermic
description: An agent, such as a herbal extrat, used for treatment of vitiligo white patches or dysfunctionalty of melanocyte cells that cause depigmentation resulting in loss of skin color with irregular white, milky white and pink patches.SAME

name: anti leukodermic
description: An agent, such as a herbal extract, used to treat leucoderma, or vitiligo, characterized by white patches or total loss of skin pigmentation.

name: anti leukonychic
description: An agent used to treat leukonychia, characterized by abnormal whiteness of the nails, either total or spots or streaks.

name: anti leukopenic
description: An agent that boosts production of leukocytes.

name: anti leukoplakic
description: An agent used to treat leukoplakia, charachterized by firm white patches in the oral cavity, gastrointestinal tract mucosal sites, or mucosal surfaces of the urinary tract and genitals.

name: anti leukorrheic
description: An agent, that may be control estrogen levels, used to treat leukorrhea, characterized by whitish or yellowish vaginal discharge.

name: anti lipedemic
description: An agent that promotes the reduction of lipid levels in the blood.

name: anti lipemic
description: An agent that prevents or counteracts the accumulation of fatty substances in the blood.

name: anti lipogenic
description: An agent that inhibits or controls production of lipids.

name: anti lipolytic
description: An agent that prevents breakdown of lipids.

name: anti lipoperoxidant
description: An agent that prevents peroxidation (oxidative degradation) of lipids.

name: anti lithic
description: An agent that prevents formation of calculi or promoting their dissolution.

name: anti lymphedemic
description: An agent that relieves effects of lymphedema, characterized by localized fluid retention and tissue swelling caused by a compromised lymphatic system, which normally returns interstitial fluid to the thoracic duct and then the bloodstream.

name: anti lymphocytic
description: An antibody that binds human T cells, used for example in treating acute rejection of organ transplantation.

name: anti lymphomic
description: An agent that acts against lymphatic cells that group together to form blood cell tumors.

name: anti mange
description: An agent used to treat mange, a skin disease of mammals caused by parasitic mites and occasionally communicable to humans.

name: anti manic
description: An agent or drug that stabilizes mood by controlling symptoms of mania, the abnormal psychological state of excitement.

name: anti mastalgic
description: An agent or drug that relieves symptoms of mastalgia (breat pain), or acts against the benign tumor.

name: anti mastitic
description: An agent used to treat inflammation of breast tissue, caused by an S. aureus infection in nursing mothers.

name: anti MD
description: An antobody specific to MD molecules, which act in conjunction with toll-like receptor family members.

name: anti multidrug resistance
description: An agent used against the microbes otherwise unaffected by a range of antimicrobials.

name: anti melasmic
description: An agent used to relieve effects of melasma, characterized by a tan or dark skin discoloration often during pregnancy.

name: anti metabolic
description: A substance that competes with, replaces, or antagonizes a particular metabolite; for example, ethionine is anantimetabolite of methionine.

name: anti metabolite
description: A substance that competes with, replaces, or antagonizes a particular metabolite; for example, ethionine is anantimetabolite of methionine.

name: anti metaplastic
description: An antibody specific to metaplastic carcinoma of the breast.

name: anti metrorrhagic
description: An agent that relieves abnormal bleeding of the uterus.

name: anti mitochondrial
description: An autoantibody in blood or serum, consisting of immunoglobulins formed against mitochondria,  indicative of the presence of, or the potential to develop the autoimmune disease Primary Biliary Cirrhosis.

name: anti mitogenic
description: An agent that inhibits mitosis, or cell division.

name: anti mitotic
description: An agent that inhibits mitosis, or cell division.

name: anti mononuccleotic
description: An agent that promotes reduction of the production of monocytes.

name: anti morphinic
description: An agent that inhibits the action of morphine.

name: anti morphinistic
description: A drug that prevents morphine from activating the mu opioid receptor, used in treating morphine addiction (morphinism).

name: anti monosodium-glutamate
description: An atidote for MSG poisoning, such as taurine.

name: anti mutagenic
description: An agnet that interferes with the mutagenicity of a substance. The interference can be in the form of prevention of the transformation of a mutagenic compound into mutagen, inactivation, or otherwise the prevention of Mutagen-DNA reaction.

name: anti myalgic
description: An agent that relieves pain or tenderness in one or more muscles.

name: anti mycoplasmotic
description: An agent, such as an antibiotic, that acts against Mycoplasma, such as in avian mycoplasmosis.

name: anti mycotic
description: An agent that destroys or prevents the growth of fungi.

name: anti myoatrophic
description: An agent that prevents the wasting of muscular tissue, or muscular atrophy.

name: anti myoclonic
description: An agent used to treat a disease characterized by involuntary twitching of a muscle or a group of muscles (which includes epilepsy). Some drugs include clonazepam, sodium valproate, piracetam, and primidone.

name: anti myopic
description: An agent that corrects nearsightedness (myopia), a refractive error of the eye.

name: anti narcotic
description: A sunbstance that blunts the senses, as opium,morphine, belladonna, and alcohol, that in large quantities cause produceeuphoria, stupor, or coma, and when used constantly can causehabituation or addiction. It can be used in medicine to relieve pain, cause sedation, and induce sleep.

name: anti neuroblastomic
description: An agent used against a malignant tumor composed of neuroblasts, most commonly in the adrenal gland.

name: anti neurogenic
description: An agent that impares a neural factor.

name: anti neuropathic
description: An agent that relieves symptoms such as pain caused by damage or disease affecting the somatosensory nervous system.

name: anti neurotic
description: An agent that relieves symptoms caused by a mental disorder involving distress, such as excessive anxiety, obsessional thoughts, or compulsive acts.

name: anti nitrosating
description: An agent that prevents the conversion of organic compounds into nitroso derivatives.

name: anti NKC
description: An agent, or antibody, that binds to a natural killer cell. For example, a NK cell is activated by interferons.

name: anti nyctalopic
description: An agent that enhances sight in dim light, or at night.

name: anti odontalgic
description: An agent, or drug, that relieves the pain of a toothache.

name: anti oncychomycotic
description: An agent that acts agains a fungal infection of the nails.

name: anti onychogryphotic
description: An agent that may be used to treat nail hypertrophy, characteristic of overgrowth resembling claws or a ram's horn.

name: anti ophidic
description: Another name for antivenom. A biological product used in the treatment of venomous bites or stings. Antivenom is created by milking venom from a relevant snake, spider, insect, or fish. The venom is then diluted and injected.

name: anti ophthalmic
description: Fat-soluble vitamins essential for normal vision, which prevent nightblindness or inflammation or dryness of the eyes.

name: anti orchitic
description: An agent that relieves symptoms of orchitis, such as inflammation of the testes, heavy pain or infection.

name: anti panniculopathic
description: An agent used to treat cellulite (Edematous Fibrosclerotic Panniculopathy), which is persistent subcutaneous fat that causes dimpling of the skin.

name: anti parotitic
description: An agent that relieves inflammation of the parotid gland.

name: anti peptic
description: An agent that inhibits the action of pepsin.

name: anti periodic
description: An agent that prevents regular recurrence of the symptoms of a disease, as in malaria.

name: anti periodontal
description: An agent that relieves or prevents the development of gum disease.

name: anti periodontitic
description: An agent that relieves inflammation of tissue around teeth, which causes shrinkage of the gums and loosening of the teeth.

name: anti peristaltic
description: An agent that opposes the force of peristalsis, or the waves of contraction of a tubular organ.

name: anti permeability
description: An agent that prevents the updtake of a substance by a cell, or tissue.

name: anti peroxidant
description: An agent that prevents the formation of oxygen atoms and peroxides, which may be stimulated in the body by certain toxins and infections.

name: anti peroxidative
description: An agent that counters or inhibits peroxidation, which refers to any oxidation reaction, especially of an oxide, that produces a peroxide.

name: anti peroxynitrite
description: An agent that inhibits peroxynitrite function, to ameliorate vasorelaxation of aging resistant arteries.

name: anti perspirant
description: An agent, or antimicrobial, that controls sweat and body odor by preventing sweat reaching the skin surface, or by reducing the bacteria that causes body odour.

name: anti pharyngitic
description: An agent that relieves inflammation of the pharynx.

name: anti pityriasic
description: An agent that relieves symptoms of skin diseases that cause formation of fine, branny scales.

name: anti plantar
description: An agent that relieves inflammation of the plantar fascia, that runs across the bottom of the foot and connects the heel bone to the toes.

name: anti plaque
description: An agent that removes or prevents the formation of a plaque.

name: anti platelet
description: An agent, or drug, that prevents the formation of blood clots that result from aggregation of platelets. It may be used to treat heart disease.

name: anti podagric
description: An agent, such as a nutritional factor, that acts to control uric acid metabolism, for treatment of gout (podagra).

name: anti polyp
description: An agent that acts to remove or prevent overgrowth of the inner wall of the colon or rectum.

name: anti porphyric
description: An agent that lowers the levels of porphyrins, such as by catalyzing reactions that consume these.

name: anti predatory
description: Relating to, or denoting, prevention of death by a predator.

name: anti proctalgic
description: An agent that relieves symptoms of proctalgia, such as severe episodic, rectal or sacrococcygeal pain, caused by cramping of the levator ani muscle.

name: anti progestational
description: An agent, or hormone such as estrogen, that prevents secretion of progesterone, which immediately follows ovulation.

name: anti prolactin
description: An agent that inhibits the production of prolactin, a hormone that stimulates milk production after childbirth.

name: anti proliferant
description: An agent that prevents or inhibits proliferation, or rapid reproduction of a cell, part, or organism.

name: anti proliferative
description: An agent or substance that prevents or stunts the spread of cells, especially malignant cells, into surrounding tissues.

name: anti promoter
description: An agent that blocks the action of a promoter molecule in carcinogenesis, potentially acting at any stage in a neoplastic transformation sequence. Examples are dietary fiber, vitamins A, C and E, selenium, indoles, flavones, or isothiocyanates.

name: anti prostanoid
description: An antibody that binds to prostanoid receptors.

name: anti prostatitic
description: An agent that relieves prostatitis, or inflammation of the prostate gland.

name: anti proteolytic
description: An agent that prevents digestion of proteins.

name: anti prostate-specific-antigen
description: An antibody binding to a protstate specific antigen, that may be used to detect inflammation of the prostate.

name: anti psittacotic
description: An antobody binding to Chlamydia psittaci specific antigens, a microorganism that cuases the psittacosis infectious disease.

name: anti radiation
description: An agent, such as a nutritional factor, or a drug, that acts to repair DNA damage cuase by exposure to radiation.

name: anti radicular
description: An agent that relieves inflammation or irritation of the nerve root of a tooth.

name: anti reproductive
description: An agent that prevents the ability of an organism to produce offspring.

name: anti retinitic
description: An agent that relieves inflammation of the retina of the eye (retinitis).

name: anti retinotic
description: An antibogy, or receptor for retinoic acid. Retinoic acid receptors bind as heterodimers to their target response elements in response to their ligands.

name: anti secretory
description: An agent that inhibits or diminishes secretion.

name: anti septic-shock
description: An agent, such as an antimicrobial agent, that acts against the infection causing septic shock.

name: anti shock
description: An agent that reduces the force of impact, or the effects of a shock. For example, in treatment of blood loss or fractures.

name: anti sialogogue
description: An agent that reduces the production of saliva.

name: anti sickling
description: An agent that prevents sickling of red blood cells, used to treat sickle cell anemia.

name: anti silicotic
description: An agent that relieves symptoms of or treats silicosis, an occupational lung disease caused by inhalation of crystalline silica dust, characterized by inflammation and scarring in the form of nodular lesions in the upper lobes of the lungs.

name: anti sinusitic
description: An agent that relieves cold symptoms.

name: anti spastic
description: An agent that prevents or relieves spasms.

name: anti spermatogenic
description: An agent that blocks spermatogenesis.

name: anti sprout
description: An agne thtat prevents sprouting, or germinating of seeds.

name: anti sprue
name_zh: 抗口瘡性腹瀉（抗熱帶性口炎性腹瀉）
description: An antibody present with the condition celiac sprue (or celiac disease), and autoimmune disorder of the small intestine.

name: anti staph
description: An agent, or an antimicrobial agent, that acts against specific Staphylococcus targets, a genus that causes Staph infections.

name: anti steatotic
description: An agent that prevents the retention or accumulation of lipids in a cell, or steatosis.

name: anti stomatitic
description: An agent that relieves inflammation of the mucous membrane of the mouth.

name: anti syncopic
description: An agent that relieves symptoms of fainting, such as low heart rate,  low blood pressure, or low blood flow to the brain.

name: anti tartar
description: An agent, such as pyrophosphate, that removes or prevents formation of tartar (hardened dental plaque) on teeth.

name: anti thiamin
description: Compounds that destroy thiamine in food, which occur naturally in food or are produced in food as a result of microbial or other action.

name: anti thyreotropic
description: An antibody that binds thyrotropin, a hormone secreted by the pituitary gland that regulates the production of thyroid hormones.

name: anti thyroid
description: A hormone antagonist acting upon thyroid hormones, such as to prevent hyperthyroidism.

name: anti tinnitic
description: An agent that relieves symptoms caused by tinnitus, characterized by constant noise or ringing in the ears.

name: anti TMJ
description: An agent that relieves inflammation or pain in temporomandibular joint and muscle disorders, which affect the chewing muscles and joints that connect the lower jaw to the skull.

name: anti tympanitic
description: An agent that prevents accumulation of gas or air in the intestine or peritoneal cavity, resulting in a distension of the abdomen.

name: anti uremic
description: An agent that acts to lower urea levels in the body, often a cause of kidney injury or kidney failure.

name: anti urethrotic
description: An agent that relieves inflammation of the urethra.

name: anti uricosuric
description: An agent that increases excretion of uric acid in the urine, thus reducing the concentration of uric acid in blood plasma.

name: anti urinary-tract-infection
description: An agent that acts against the infection of kidneys, ureters, bladder, or urethra (the structures that urine passes through before being eliminated from the body).

name: anti uveitic
description: An agent that relieves inflammation of the uvea.

name: anti vitiligic
description: An agent that prevents loss of skin pigmentation, characteristic of vitiligo.

name: anti wart
description: An agent that removes or diminishes warts.

name: anti wrinkle
description: An agent that prevents or diminishes the formation of wrinckles, often a factor of aging.

name: anti X-radiation
description: An agent that prevents negative effects of x-ray overexposure such as skin erythema.

name: anti xerotic
description: An agent that relieves or diminishes abnormal dryness of the skin, eyes, or mucous membranes (xerosis), or that may diminish the normal hardening of aging tissue.

name: aphicide
description: An insecticide used against aphids.

name: aphrodisiac
description: A sunstance, as food, drink, or drug, that stimulates sexual desire.

name: apoptotic
description: An agent that stimulates the process of apoptosis, or programmed cell death.

name: artemicide
description: An agent that kills weeds of the Artemisia genus.

name: arteriodilator
description: An agent that acts to dilate arteries to increase blood flow in the heart, or away form the heart.

name: ataractic
description: An agent that has a tranquilizing effect.

name: atherogenic
description: An agent that promotes the formation of fatty plaques in the arteries.

name: ATPase stimulant
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of ATPases, a class of enzymes that catalyze the decomposition of ATP into ADP and a free phosphate ion.

name: autotoxic
description: An substance secreted by a species into the environment, that inhibits growth or reproduction of members of that same species.

name: beta-blocker
description: An agent, or drug, that lowers blood pressure, by blocking the effects of the hormone epinephrine, also known as adrenaline.

name: bifidogenic
description: A compound that enhances the growth of bifidobacteria in either a product or in the intestines of humans and/or animals.

name: bilirubinolytic
description: An agent that converts indirect bilirubin into direct bilirubin (in the liver), a soluble form that is excreted in feces.

name: bitter
description: A substance that has a sharp, pungent taste or smell.

name: bronchorrheic
description: Pertaining to hemorrhage of the bronchi.

name: bruchiphobe
description: Pertaining to organisms of the genus Brucella.

name: calcium-sparing
description: An agent that limits calcium excretion in urine, a diuretic.

name: candidicide
description: Pertaining to an organism of the Candida genus, such as Candida albicans.

name: candidistat
description: An agent inhibits growth of organisms of the genus Candida , such as Candida albicans.

name: capillarifortificant
description: An agent that strengthens blood capilleries.

name: capillarigenic
description: An agent that promotes formation, or proliferation of capillaries.

name: capillariprotective
description: An agent that acts to alleviate or prevent certain conditions of the capillaries. An example is the protective effect of astaxanthin on capillary regression.

name: carminative
description: An herb or preparation intended to either prevent formation of gas in the gastrointestinal tract or facilitate the expulsion of said gas, thereby combatting flatulence.

name: caspase-3 inducer
description: An apoptosis inducing agent that stimulates the activity of caspase-3, allowing it to interact with caspace-8 and caspace-9.

name: caspase-8 inducer
description: An apoptosis inducing agent that directly stimulates the activity of caspase-8, a member of the cysteine proteases, which are implicated in apoptosis and cytokine processing.

name: catabolic
description: A process, or reaction, that relesases energy which is used by anabolic reactions.

name: cataractagenic
description: An agent that induces the formation of cataracts.

name: catecholaminogenic
description: A chemical which functions to directly modulate the catecholamine systems in the body or brain. Examples include adrenergics and dopaminergics.

name: cerebrodepressant
description: An agent, or drug, that has a sedating effect on the brain, reducing activity and alertness and, in some instances, causing a loss of consciousness.

name: cerebroprotective
description: An agent or mechanism that alleviates or prevents certain conditions of the brain. An example is the autoregulation of cerebral blood flow aimed at improving neuronal survival in a changing environment.

name: cerebrostimulant
description: An agent, or drug, that causes an increase in activity of the central nervous system, providing a temporary sense of alertness and well-being as well as relief from fatigue.

name: cerebrotonic
description: Relating to or resembling a personality type characterized by shyness, introspection, and emotional restraint, and often associated with an ectomorphic physique.

name: cerumenolytic
description: An agent that softens serumen (earwax) for removal.

name: cestodicide
description: An agent that kills cestoda, parasitic flatworms, of the phylum Platyhelminthes.

name: chitinolytic
description: Of, pertaining to, or relating to the enzyme converting chitin (a polysaccharide) into chitobiose (a disaccharide).

name: chitosanolytic
description: An agent, or enzyme, that catalyzes breakdown of chitosan.

name: cholagogue
description: An agent that promotes the discharge of bile from the system, purging it downward.

name: choleretic
description: An agent, or substance, that increases the volume of secretion of bile from the liver as well as the amount of solids secreted.

name: cholesterolytic
description: An agent, or enzyme, that catalyzes breakdown of cholesterol.

name: choline-sparing
description: An agent that prevents the excretion of cholin in urine, a water-soluble nutrient grouped within the B-complex vitamins.

name: chronotropic
description: An agent, or drug, that change the heart rate by affecting the nerves controlling the heart, or by changing the rhythm produced by the sinoatrial node.

name: cicatrizant
description: An agent that promotes healing through the formation of scar tissue. For example, sandalwood is a natural cicatrizant.

name: ciliotoxic
description: An agent that is toxic to the cilia in the lungs.

name: circulatory stimulant
description: An agent that increases blood flow to and from tissues and organs. Circulatory stimulants are an important part of holistic treatment helping the body to rectify imbalances by bringing nutrients to an area and taking away toxins.

name: clastogenic
name_zh: 致斷裂（染色體斷裂誘發）
description: A mutagenic agent giving rise to or inducing disruption or breakages of chromosomes, leading to sections of the chromosome being deleted, added, or rearranged.

name: coagulant
description: An agent that causes blood or another liquid to coagulate.

name: cold preventive
description: An agent, drug or nutritional factor, that prevents development of cold symptoms, such as congestion or body aches.

name: coleoptophile
description: A substance able to kill or inhibit the activity of beetles.

name: collagenic
description: An agent that stimulates production of collage, the fibrous protein constituent of bone, cartilage, tendon, and other connective tissue.

name: collyrium
description: A lotion or liquid wash used as a cleanser for the eyes, particularly in diseases of the eye.

name: comedogenic
description: An agent that causes blackheads by blocking the pores of the skin.

name: comedolytic
description: An agent that resolves comedones or prevents the formation of new comedones (skin-coloured, small bumps (papules) frequently found on the forehead and chin of those with acne).

name: congestant
description: An agent that causes congestion, which is the act of gathering into a heap or mass (accumulation).

name: controlled
description: The behaviour of which is supervised or regulated.

name: convulsant
description: An agent, or drug that induces convulsions and/or epileptic seizures, the opposite of an anticonvulsant. These drugs generally act as stimulants at low doses, but are not used for this purpose due to the risk of convulsions and consequent excitotoxicity.

name: coronarydilator
description: An agent, or drug, that dilates the coronary vessels.

name: corrosive
description: A substance that destroys or damages other substances with which it comes into contact. It may attack a great variety of materials, including metals and various organic compounds, but people are mostly concerned with its effects on living tissue: it causes chemical burns on contact.

name: corticosterogenic
description: An agent that stimulates the production of corticosterone, a 21-carbon steroid hormone of the corticosteroid type produced in the cortex of the adrenal glands in rodents and other non-human animals.

name: counterirritant
description: An agent that creates irritation or mild inflammation in one location with the goal of lessening discomfort and/or inflammation in another location.

name: crustacicide
description: An agent that kills organisms of the subphylum Crustacea.

name: culicide
description: An agent that destroys mosquitoes.

name: curare
description: A plant extract alkaloid poison that inhibits the nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR), which is a subtype of acetylcholine receptor found at the neuromuscular junction. This causes weakness of the skeletal muscles and, when administered in a sufficient dose, eventual death by asphyxiation due to paralysis of the diaphragm.

name: cyanogenic
description: An agent that stimulates producing hydrogen cyanide.

name: cyclooxygenase activator
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of cyclooxygenase, an enzyme that is responsible for formation of prostanoids, including prostaglandins such as prostacyclin and thromboxane.

name: cytochrome-p450 inducer
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of cytochrome-p450 enzymes, hemoproteins that act for example as terminal oxidase enzymes in electron transfer chains.

name: cytochrome-p488 inducer
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of cytochrome-p488 enzymes, isoenzymes of cytochrome-p450.

name: cytoprotective
description: An agent that protects cells from noxious chemicals or other stimuli.

name: cytostatic
description: An agent that inhibits cell growth.

name: decalcifier
description: An agent, or solution, that has the ability to remove calcium or calcium compounds form a target, such as bones or teeth.

name: degranulant
description: An agent that stimulates the loss or release of granules of a substance, typically as part of an immune reaction.

name: deliriant
description: A class of hallucinogen that are unique in that, even with lower doses, they offer solid hallucinations which display themselves seamlessly into waking consciousness, similar to fully formed dreams or delusions.

name: demulcent
description: An agent that forms a soothing film over a mucous membrane, relieving minor pain and inflammation of the membrane.

name: dentifrice
description: An agent used along with a toothbrush to clean and polish natural teeth.

name: deodorant
description: A substance that removes or conceals unpleasant smells, especially bodily odors.

name: depigmentor
description: A pigment suppresing agent that has a lightening effect on skin.

name: depressant
description: An agent, or drug that lowers neurotransmission levels, which is to depress or reduce arousal or stimulation, in various areas of the brain.

name: depressor
description: A muscle whose contraction pulls down the part of the body to which it is attached, or a nerve whose stimulation results in a lowering of blood pressure.

name: dermatitigenic
description: An agent that causes inflammation of the skin (dermatitis).

name: desmutagenic
name_zh: 去致突變（降低致突變性）
description: An agent, or compound, acting directly on a mutagen to decrease its mutagenicity.

name: diaphoretic
description: An agent, or drug, that induces perspiration.

name: digestive
description: An agent that aids with digestion, the breakdown of food into smaller and smaller components which can be absorbed and assimilated into the body.

name: DNA-binder
description: An agent, or protein, that is composed of DNA-binding domains and thus has a specific or general affinity for either single or double stranded DNA.

name: DNA-protective
description: An agent, such as a herbal extract, that ameliorates or prevents DNA damage in an organism.

name: ecbolic
description: An agent that induces contractions of the uterus leading to expulsion of a fetus.

name: edemagenic
description: An agent that elicits edema by causing capillary damage but not the cellular response of true inflammation.

name: emetic
description: An agent that causes vomiting.

name: emmenagogue
description: An agent, or herb, that stimulates blood flow in the pelvic area and uterus; some stimulate menstruation.

name: emollient
description: An agent that softens or soothes the skin.

name: encephalopathic
description: An agent that causes a disorder or disease of the brain.

name: endocrinactive
description: A sunstance that interacts or interferes with normal hormonal action. When this leads to adverse effects, these substances are called endocrine disruptors.

name: endorphinogenic
description: A substance that induces locus coeruleus hyperactivity, causing an increase in norepinephrine release and activity.

name: energizer
description: An agent that supplies energy, typically kinetic, to another.

name: enterocontractant
description: An agent that stimulates muscle contractions of the intestine.

name: enteromotility enhancer
description: An agent that stimulates movement of food through the intestine.

name: enterorelaxant
description: An agent that reduces or inhibits the movement of muscles of the intestine.

name: epidermal stimulant
description: An agent that stimulates renewal of the epidermis, used to ameliorate wrinkles or other aging effects.

name: epileptigenic
description: An agnet that causes an epileptic attack.

name: ergogenic
description: An agent that enhances athletic performance or facilitate physical exercise. Examples are performance-enhancing drugs, mechanical aids, physiological aids, nutritional aids (sports supplements), and psychological aids.

name: ergotamine enhancer
description: An agent that stimulates production of ergotamine, a fungal secondary metabolite that also has biological activity as a vasoconstrictor.

name: erythrocytolytic
description: An agent that destroys red blood cells, by hemolysin activity.

name: essential
description: An agent, substance, or compound, that is absolutely necessary for a certain mechanism or condition.

name: estrogenic
description: An agent that has an action similar to that of an estrogen.

name: ethanolytic
description: Lytic effect of ethanol on target cells, for instance by disrupting cell wall peptidoglycan of Escherichia coli.

name: euphoriant
description: An agent, or drug, that produces a feeling of euphoria.

name: fatal
description: An agent, or condition, that causes death.

name: feline attractant
description: An odorant that has an effect on cat behavior. A cat presented with a cat attractant may roll in it, paw at it, or chew on the source of the smell.

name: fibrinolytic
description: An agent that breaks down blood clots, naturally occuring or disease caused.

name: flatugenic
description: An agent that induces flatulence, or accumulation of gas in the digestive system.

name: flatulent
description: Related to or causing an accumulation of gas in the alimentary canal.

name: fungistat
description: A substance that inhibits the growth of fungi.

name: gastroirritant
description: Food that causes irritation to the stomach lining.

name: glaucomagenic
description: An agent that casues formation of glaucoma, a condition associated with damage of the optic nerve due to buildup of pressure inside the eye.

name: glutathionergic
description: The effect of glutathione activates nuptial behavior, spawning, and electrophysiological activity, for instance in Nereis succinea.

name: glutathionigenic
description: An agent that stimulates glutathione synthesis.

name: goitrogenic
description: The effect of goitrogens on the thyroid gland. Goitrogens are substances (whether in drugs, chemicals, or foods) that disrupt the production of thyroid hormones by interfering with iodine uptake in the thyroid gland. This triggers the pituitary to release TSH, which then promotes the growth of thyroid tissue, eventually leading to goiter.

name: gonadotropic
description: An agent that affects the development or activity of the ovary or testis.

name: glutathione-S-transferase inducer
name_zh: 穀胱甘肽S-轉移酶誘導劑
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of glutathione S-transferase enzymes.

name: glucuronosyltransferase inducer
name_zh: 葡萄糖醛酸轉移酶誘導劑
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of glucuronosyltransferase, an enzyme that catalyzes the transfer of the glucuronic acid component of UDP-glucuronic acid to a small hydrophobic molecule,  a glucuronidation reaction.

name: hallucinogenic
description: An agent that causes hallucinations, perception anomalies, and other substantial subjective changes in thoughts, emotion, and consciousness.

name: HDL-genic
description: An agent that stimulates production of HDL, or 'good cholesterol'.

name: hematopoietic
description: Pertaining to hematopoiesis, the production of all types of blood cells.

name: hemoglobin inducer
description: An agent that stimulates production of hemoglobin.

name: hemolytic
description: An agent that ruptures red blood cells.

name: hemopoietic
description: An agent that induces formation of red blood cells.

name: hemostat
description: An agent that controls bleeding.

name: hemostatic
description: An agent that arrests the flow of blood within the vessels.

name: hepatomagenic
description: Related to, or causing, abnormal growth of liver tissue or cancer.

name: hepatotropic
description: An agent that has a special affinity for or exerting a specific effect on the liver.

name: herbistat
description: A herbal preparation used for skin ailments, such as cuts, bites or infections.

name: hirudicide
description: An agent destructive to leeches.

name: histaminic
description: Pertaining to histamines,  physiologically active amines found in plant and animal tissue and released from mast cells as part of an allergic reaction in humans. They stimulates gastric secretion and cause dilation of capillaries, constriction of bronchial smooth muscle, and decreased blood pressure.

name: hypercholesterolemic
description: Pertaining to, or having the condition hypercholesterolemia, the presence of excessive cholesterol in the blood.

name: hyperemic
description: An agent that increases the quantity of blood flow to a body part (engorgement)

name: hyperpyrexic
description: Pertaining to, or having, extremely high fever.

name: hypertensive
description: Pertaining to, or having, high blood pressure, or arterial hypertension.

name: hyperthermic
description: Pertaining to, or having, unusually high body temperature.

name: hyperthyroid
description: A disorder in which the thyroid gland makes and releases more thyroid hormone than the body needs.

name: hypertrichotic
description: Pertaining to, or having, abnormal amount of hair growth over the body.

name: hyperuricemic
description: An agent that increases uric acid levels in the blood.

name: hypnotic
description: An agent, such as a psychoactive drug, that induces sleep and is used in the treatment of insomnia (sleeplessness), or surgical anesthesia.

name: hypoammonemic
description: Pertaining to, or having, abnormally high levels of ammonia in the blood, a dangerous condition that may lead to encephalopathy and death.

name: hypocalcemic
description: Pertaining to, or having, low serum calcium levels in the blood.

name: hypocholesterolemic
description: Pertaining to, or having, abnormally low levels of cholesterol in the blood.

name: hypokalemic
description: Pertaining to, or having, abnormally low potassium levels in the blood.

name: hypolipemic
description: An agent, or drug, used to treat hyperlipidemias by lowering lipid levels.

name: hypolipidemic
description: An agent, or drug, used to treat hyperlipidemias by lowering lipid levels.

name: hypotensive
description: Pertaining to, or having, abnormally low blood pressure.

name: hypothalmic depressant
description: An agent that reduces hypothalamus activity, the portion of the brain that controls activity of the pituitary gland.

name: hypothermic
description: Pertaining to, or having, abnormally low body temperature.

name: hypotriglyceridemic
description: Pertaining to, or having, abnormally low levels of triglycerides in the blood.

name: hypouricemic
description: Pertaining to, or having, abnormally low levels of uric acid in the blood.

name: ileorelaxant
description: An agent that reduces the muscular action of the ileum.

name: inflammatory
description: An agent that causes inflammation of a part of the body.

name: inhalant
description: A substance whose volatile vapors or gases are taken in via the nose and trachea.

name: inotropic
name_zh: 肌力變性（心肌收縮力調節）
description: An agent that alters the force or energy of muscular contractions. Negatively inotropic agents weaken the force of muscular contractions. Positively inotropic agents increase the strength of muscular contraction.

name: insulin-sparing
description: An agent, that in the presence of insulin, reduces the dose of insulin required to achieve a given level of glycemic control.

name: insulinogenic
description: An agent that promotes the production and release of insulin by the islets of Langerhans in the pancreas.

name: interferonogenic
description: An agent that stimulates the production and release of interferons, cytokine signaling proteins released by a host cell in response to the presence of pathogens, used by the organism for inter-cellular communication to trigger defence mechanisms of the immune system.

name: irritant
description: An agent that causes slight inflammation or other discomfort to the body.

name: juvabional
description: Pertaining to the activity of juvenile hormones, a group of acyclic sesquiterpenoids that regulate many aspects of insect physiology, such as development, reproduction, diapause, and polyphenisms. Juvenile hormones ensure growth of the larva, while preventing metamorphosis.

name: keratolytic
description: An agent that removes warts and other lesions in which the epidermis produces excess skin.

name: lachrymatory
description: An agent that causes secretion of tears (lachrymation).

name: lactagogue
description: An agent that promotes the flow of milk.

name: larvicide
description: An insecticide that is specifically targeted against the larval life stage of an insect.

name: larvistat
description: An agent that inhibits development of larvae, for exmaple to reduce a mosquito population.

name: LDL lytic
description: An agent that stimulates breakdown of LDL, or 'bad cholesterol'.

name: leptingenic
description: An agent that stimulates synthesis of leptin, a hormone that regulates energy balance by inhibiting hunger.

name: leucocyte depressant
description: An agent that lowers the activity of leukocytes, white blood cells involved in counteracting foreign substances and disease.

name: leucocytogenic
description: An agent that stimulates formation of leucocytes.

name: lipase inducer
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of lipase, and enzyme that catalyzes the hydrolysis of fats (lipids).

name: lipolytic
description: An agent that stimulates hydrolysis of fats into fatty acids and glycerol.

name: lipotropic
description: A compound that helps break down and metabolize fat in the body.

name: lithogenic
description: An agent that promotes the the formation of calculi.

name: litholytic
description: An agent that dissolves calculi.

name: lubricant
description: An agent, or substance, such as oil or grease, used for minimizing friction.

name: lymphocytogenic
description: An agent that stimulates production of lymphocytes, or malignant lymphocytes.

name: lymphokinetic
description: Pertaining to central movement, or flow, of lymph. The flow of lymph is refered to as lymphokinesis.

name: maltase-promoter
description: A promoter that initiates transcription of the maltase gene. Maltase catalyzes the hydrolysis of maltose to the simple sugar glucose. This enzyme is found in plants, bacteria, and yeast.

name: mast-cell stabilizer
description: An agent, such as a cromone medication, used to prevent or control certain allergic disorders. They block a calcium channel essential for mast cell degranulation,stabilizing the cell and thereby preventing the release of histamine and related mediators.

name: mastogenic
description: The natural swelling and growth of breast tissues.

name: melaninogenic
description: An agent that stimulates production of melanin, which determines skin and hair color and is naturally  present in the skin to varying degrees.

name: melatoninogenic
description: An agent that stimulates production of melatonin, a hormone that anticipates the daily onset of darkness.

name: membranolytic
description: An agent that disrupts a biological membrane.

name: metastatic
description: Having to do with metastasis, which is the spread of cancer from the primary site (place where it started) to other places in the body.

name: microvasculokinetic
description: Pertaining to motion of the microvasculature, the portion of the vasculature of the body consisting of the smaller vessels.

name: mineral-corticoid
description: A corticosteroid hormone, which is synthesized by the adrenal cortex. Aldosterone, the main mineralocorticoid, is necessary for regulation of salt and water in the body. It increases sodium re-absorption by an action on the distal tubules of the kidney.

name: mitogenic
description: A substance, usually some form of a protein, that encourages a cell to commence cell division, triggering mitosis.

name: mitotic
description: Pertaining to mitosis, a process of nuclear division in eukaryotic cells that occurs when a parent cell divides to produce two identical daughter cells.

name: monoamine precursor
description: An aromatic amino acid (like phenylalanine, tyrosine, tryptophan), from which a monoamine is derived. Monoamines are neurotransmitters like serotonin, norepinephrine, and dopamine.

name: monoaminergic
description: An agent which functions to directly modulate the serotonin, dopamine, norepinephrine/epinephrine, and/or melatonin neurotransmitter systems in the body or brain.

name: motor depressant
description: An agent, or drug, that reduces the normal functioning level of motor neurons, mainly in voluntary muscles.

name: motor stimulant
description: An agent, or drug, that increases the normal functioning level of motor neurons.

name: mucogenic
description: An agent that promotes formation of mucus.

name: mucokinetic
description: An agent that aids in the clearance of mucus from the airways, lungs, bronchi, and trachea.

name: mucotropic
description: An agent, inhaled, that augments the volume output of respiratory tract fuild.

name: musculotropic
description: An agent that acts on muscle tissue, particularly with a stimulating effect.

name: mutagenic
description: An agent, such as a chemical, ultraviolet light, or a radioactive element, that has the ability to induce or increase the frequency of mutation in an organism.

name: mycobactericide
description: An agent that kills bacteria of the genus Mycobacteria.

name: mycoplasmicide
description: An agent that kills bacteria of the genus Mycoplasma.

name: mycoplasmistat
description: An agent that inhibits growth of bacteria of the Mycoplasma genus.

name: myocontractant
description: An agent that stimulates contractions of smooth muscle.

name: myopic
description: Pertaining to, or having, nearsightedness (myopia).

name: myoprotective
description: An agent that acts to alleviate or prevent certain condition affecting smooth muscle.

name: myorelaxant
description: An agent, or drug, that reduces muscle contractility by blocking the transmission of nerve impulses or by decreasing the excitability of the motor end plate or by other actions

name: myostimulant
description: An agent that stimulates an increase of the rhythm and the amplitude of smooth muscle movement.

name: myotonic
description: Pertaining to, or having, delayed relaxation (prolonged contraction) of the skeletal muscles after voluntary contraction or electrical stimulation (myotonia).

name: natriuretic
description: Pertaining to the process of excretion of sodium in the urine via action of the kidneys.

name: nematistat
description: An effect which have been observed when nematodes are exposed to low concentrations of organophosphates and carbamates. These products do not always kill nematodes, but at times appear to disorient, paralyze, or confuse nematodes and so prevent infestation of roots.

name: neoplastic
description: An agent, or effect, that results in abnormal growth of tissue.

name: neurodepressant
description: An agent that reduces neural signal transmisission.

name: neuroexcitant
description: An agent that increases neural signal transmisission.

name: neurogenic
description: Something caused by, controlled by, or arising in the nervous system.

name: neuroleptic
description: An agent, or drug, that reduces nervous tension by depressing nerve functions.

name: neurolytic
description: Pertaining to, or causing, disintegration of nerve tissue.

name: neuromyostimulant
description: An agent that increases activity of smooth muscle nerves.

name: neuroparalytic
description: Something relating to, causing, or characterized by paralysis or loss of sensation due to a lesion in a nerve.

name: neuropathogenic
description: An agent capable of causing disease of nervous tissue.

name: neurostimulant
description: An agent that quickens the functional activity of neurons.

name: neurotropic
description: An agent, such as a virus, toxin, or chemical, that attacks or affects the nervous system preferentially.

name: NF-kappa-B inducer
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of NF-kappa-B, a protein complex that controls transcription of DNA, cytokine production and cell survival.

name: nitric-oxide-genic
description: Pertaining to increased levels if nitric oxide in the blood, which acts as a vasodilator.

name: nitric-oxide scavenger
description: An agent that retains nitric oxide. An example is nitric oxide scavenging by red blood cells.

name: nociceptive
description: Somehting induced by, or responding to a nociceptive stimulus, a harmful stimuli in the nervous system.

name: nodulation signal
description: Aignaling molecules produced by bacteria known as rhizobia during the initiation of nodules on the root of legumes. A symbiosis is formed when legumes take up the bacteria.

name: occuloirritant
description: An agent that causes irritation of the eye.

name: odontolytic
description: An agne that promotes resorption of dental tissue.

name: ophthalmic antialkali
description: An agent that relieves effects of an alkaline burn of the eye.

name: osmoregulator
description: An organism that can regulate or keep the solutes or salts of its body fluid at a higher or lower concentration than the concentration of solutes in the external medium, although this regulation may be limited at extremely high or extremely low external solute concentrations.

name: osteoblastogenic
description: An agent that stimulates production of osteoblasts.

name: osteogenic
description: Something derived from or composed of any tissue concerned in bone growth or repair.

name: osteolytic
description: Pertaining to the dissolution of bone, especially the loss of calcium from bone.

name: ovicide
description: An agent that kills eggs, especially an insecticide effective against the egg stage.

name: oviposition stimulant
description: An agent that stimulates the laying of eggs, especially by the ovipositor, a tubular structure, usually concealed but sometimes extending outside the abdomen, with which many female insects deposit eggs.

name: ovulant
description: An agent that stimulates ovulation, the release of an ovum from an ovary.

name: oxyuricide
description: A substance that destroys pinworms.

name: ozone scavenger
description: An agent that retains ozone from a gas stream .

name: cytochrome-P450-1A inducer
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of cytochrome-P450-1A, a cytochrome-P450 enzyme involved in phase I xenobiotic and drug metabolism, as well as metabolic activation of aromatic hydrocarbons.

name: cytochrome-P450-2B inducer
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of cytochrome-P450-2B a cytochrome-P450 that catalyzes many reactions involved in drug metabolism (such as the anti-cancer drugs cyclophosphamide and ifosphamide) and synthesis of cholesterol, steroids and other lipids.

name: pancreatogenic
description: Somehting of pancreatic origin, or formed in the pancreas.

name: paralytic
description: Related to, or having, paralysis, which is loss or impairment of the ability to move a body part, usually as a result of damage to its nerve supply.

name: paramecicide
description: An agent that kills unicellular ciliated protozoans of the genus Paramecium.

name: parasiticide
description: A substance used in medicine and veterinary medicine to kill parasites (especially those other than bacteria or fungi).

name: parasympathomimetic
description: A substance, or drug, that stimulates the parasympathetic nervous system. It can act either directly by stimulating the nicotinic or muscarinic receptors (thus mimicking acetylcholine), or indirectly by inhibiting cholinesterase, promoting acetylcholine release, or other mechanisms.

name: pectinolytic
description: An agent that induces hydrolysis of pectins.

name: pemphigenic
description: An agent that induces formation of fluid-filled blisters on the skin (pemphigoid), an auto-immune disorder.

name: percutaneostimulant
description: A stimulant that is effective through the skin.

name: peristaltic
description: An agnet that stimulates peristalsis, a series of muscle contractions that occur in the digestive tract that moves food through the digestive system.

name: peroxynitrite scavenger
description: An agne that binds to and removes peroxynitrite. An example is uric acid.

name: phagocytotic
description: Having the ability to engulf a solid or a cell, through phagocytosis, a form of endocytosis involving vascular internalization.

name: photoactive
description: Having the ability to respond to light photoelectrically, or to respond to sunlight or ultraviolet radiation by chemical reaction.

name: photodermatitigenic
description: An agent, or allergen, which is activated by light to sensitize the allergic response, which causes a rash or other systemic effects upon exposure.

name: photosensitizer
description: A molecule that produces a chemical change in another molecule in a photochemicalprocess. Photosensitizers are commonly used in polymer chemistry in reactions such as photopolymerization, photocrosslinking, and photodegradation.Photosensitizers generally act by absorbing energy electromagnetic radiation in the form of ultraviolet or visible light and transferring it to adjacent molecules.

name: phototoxic
description: An agent that renders the skin susceptible to damage (as sunburn or blisters) upon exposure to light and especially ultraviolet light.

name: phytotoxic
description: An agent poisonous to plants, having a toxic effect on plant growth.

name: piscicide
description: A substance poisonous to fish. The primary use for piscicides is to eliminate a dominant species of fish in a body of water, as the first step in attempting to populate the body of water with a different fish. They are also used to combat parasitic and invasive species of fish.

name: pituitary stimulant
description: An agent that stimulates hormone production by the pituitary gland. Hormones secreted from the pituitary gland help control: growth, blood pressure, certain functions of the sex organs, thyroid glands and metabolism as well as some aspects of pregnancy, childbirth, nursing, water/salt concentration and the kidneys, temperature regulation and pain relief.

name: plasmodicide
description: An agent that kills malaria parasites.

name: podagragenic
description: Pertaining to formation of podagra, painful condition of the big toe, caused by gout.

name: potassium-sparing
description: An agent, or diuretic drug, that prevents the secretion of potassium into the urine. It may be used in the treatment of hypertension and management of congestive heart failure.

name: poultice
description: A soft, moist mass of material, typically of plant material or flour, applied to the body to relieve soreness and inflammation.

name: prebiotic
description: Non-digestible fiber compounds that pass undigested through the upper part of the gastrointestinal tract and stimulate the growth or activity of advantageous bacteria that colonize the large bowel by acting as substrate for them.

name: pressor
description: An agent htat increases blood pressure by stimulating constriction of the blood vessels.

name: priapistic
description: Related to, or having, persistent, usually painful erection of the penis, especially as a consequence of disease and not related to sexual arousal.

name: probiotic
description: A microorganism that provides health benefits. Commonly claimed benefits of probiotic supplements include the decrease of potentially pathogenic gastrointestinal microorganisms, the reduction of gastrointestinal discomfort, the strengthening of the immune system, the improvement of the skin's function, the improvement of bowel regularity, the strengthening of the resistance to cedar pollen allergens, the decrease in body pathogens, the reduction of flatulence and bloating, the protection of DNA, the protection of proteins and lipids from oxidative damage, and the maintaining of individual intestinal microbiota in subjects receiving antibiotic treatment.

name: progestational
description: Something that precedes pregnancy or gestation. Related to, inducing, or constituting the modifications of the female mammalian system associated especially with ovulation and corpus luteum formation.

name: progesteronigenic
description: An agent that stimulates production of progesterone, a progestogen sex hormone involved in the menstrual cycle, pregnancy, and embryogenesis of humans and other species.

name: pro HLDL
description: An agent that stimulates or enhances the activity of HLDL (Human Low Density Lipoprotein), which facilitates the movement of fats and cholesterol within the water based solution of the blood stream.

name: prolactinogenic
description: Pertaining to production of the hormone prolactin, which may lead to breast enlargement.

name: proliferant
description: A mildly irritating substance used in sclerotherapy to strengthen weakened connective tissues.

name: prooxidant
description: An endobiotic or xenobiotic that induces oxidative stress either by generation of ROS or by inhibiting antioxidant systems. It can include all reactive, free radical containing molecules in cells or tissues.

name: prostaglandigenic
description: Pertaining to production of prostaglandins, a group of cyclic fatty acid compounds with varying hormonelike effects, notably the promotion of uterine contractions.

name: prostatitigenic
description: Pertaining to inflammation of the prostate gland.

name: proteolytic
description: An agent that catalyzes proteolysis, the breakdown of proteins into smaller polypeptides or amino acids.

name: protisticide
description: An agent, which can be a herbicide, that kills protozoans.

name: pruritogenic
description: An agent that causes pruritus, or itching.

name: pulmonoprotective
description: An agent that acts to alleviate or prevent certain conditions affecting lung tissue.

name: pyrogenic
description: An agent that induces fever, by generating heat.

name: quinone-reductase inducer
description: An agent that stimulates quinone reductase enzymatic activity, also a biomarker for cancer chemoprevention.

name: renoirritant
description: An agent that causes irritation or inflammation of kydney tissue.

name: repellent
description: An agent able to repel a particular thing, impervious to a particular substance.

name: respiradepressant
description: An agent that supresses gas exchange, resulting in an increase of carbon dioxide concentrations and respiratory acidosis. Respiratory depression is characteristic of hypoventilation.

name: respiraparalytic
description: Related to, or having, respiratory paralysis, which occurs when the muscles associated with breathing become too weak to function properly.

name: respirasensitizer
description: A substance that will induce hypersensitivity of the airways following inhalation of the substance.

name: respirastimulant
description: An agent that stimulates an increase in tidal volume, and respiratory rate.

name: respirodepressant
description: An agent that diminishes ventilation, the movement of air between the environment and the lungs via inhalation and exhalation.

name: respirostimulant
description: An agent that increases ventilation, the movement of air between the environment and the lungs via inhalation and exhalation.

name: retinoprotectant
description: An agent that acts to alleviate or prevent certain condition affecting the retina.

name: ribosome inactivator
description: A protein that is a protein synthesis inhibitor which acts at the ribosome.

name: roborant
description: An agnet, or drug, that restores vigor or strength.

name: rubefacient
description: A substance for topical application that produces redness of the skin, by causing dilation of the capillaries and an increase in blood circulation.

name: schistosomicide
description: An agent, or drug, destructive to schistosomes, blood flukes transmitted by snails to human hosts in many parts of Africa, Brazil, and Asia. Praziquantel, albendazole, niridazole, metrifonate, oxamniquine hycanthone hydrochloride, and various salts of antimony, including stibophen, are potent antischistosomal agents.

name: schizophrenigenic
description: An agent that has an effect on, or causes schizophrenia, a mental disorder often characterized by abnormal social behavior and failure to recognize what is real.

name: sclerosant
description: An injectable irritant that is used in the treatment of varicose veins and that causes inflammation and subsequentfibrosis, thus obliterating the lumen of the vein.

name: seasoning
description: Enhancement of flavor by adding salt, herbs, or spices to food.

name: secretogogue
description: A substance that promotes secretion.

name: secretolytic
description: An agent that increases the production of serous mucus in the respiratory tract.

name: secretomotor
description: A structure, often a nerve, that has the capacity to induce a gland to secrete a substance, usually mucus or serous fluid.

name: sialogogue
description: An agent that stimulates the flow of saliva.

name: sodium-sparing
description: An agent that prevents excretion of sodium in the urine.

name: soporific
description: An agent, or drug, that induces drowsiness or sleep.

name: spasmogenic
description: An agnent that causes spasms, which are sudden involuntary muscular contraction or convulsive movement.

name: spasmolytic
description: An agent, or drug, that relieves spasm of smooth muscle.

name: spermicide
description: A contraceptive substance that destroys sperm, inserted vaginally prior to intercourse to prevent pregnancy.

name: spermigenic
description: An agent that induces sperm production.

name: spice
description: A seed, fruit, root, bark, berry, bud or vegetable substance primarily used for flavoring, coloring or preserving food. Spices are distinguished from herbs, which are parts of leafy green plants used for flavoring or as a garnish. Many spices have antimicrobial properties.

name: spinoconvulsant
description: An agent that induces convulsion and/or epileptic seizures affecting spinal cord nerves.

name: sternutatory
description: An agent, or substance that causes sneezing. Pepper is an exmaple.

name: stimulant
description: An agent, or drug, that induces temporary improvements in either mental or physical functions or both. Examples of these kinds of effects may include enhanced alertness, wakefulness, and locomotion, among others.

name: stomachic
description: An agnet, or drug, that promotes the appetite or assists digestion.

name: styptic
description: A sunbstance capable of causing bleeding to stop when it is applied to a wound.

name: sucrase promoter
