description: An agent, or inducer, that activates sucrases, enzymes located on the brush border of the small intestine that catalyze the hydrolysis of sucrose to fructose and glucose.

name: sudorific
description: An agent, or drug, that induces sweating.

name: suppository
description: A drug delivery system that is inserted into the rectum, vagina, or urethra, where it dissolves or melts and is absorbed into the blood stream.

name: suppurative
description: An agent that promotes formation of pus. Pertaining to or emanating from suppuration.

name: synergist
description: A substance, organ, or other agent that participates in an effect of synergy, which refers to the creation of a whole that is greater than the simple sum of its parts.

name: systolic depressant
description: An agent that reduces systolic blood pressure, which is the pressure created as the heart contracts and pushes blood through the arteries.

name: taenicide
description: An agent, or drug, that destroys tapeworms.

name: teratologic
description: Pertaining to deformity, either congenital or resulting from accident.

name: termiticide
description: An agent that kills termites, eusocial insects that are classified at the taxonomic rank of infraorder Isoptera, or as epifamily Termitoidae within the cockroach order Blattodea.

name: testosterogenic
description: An agent that promotes production of testosterone. It may be used in treating premature ejaculation or impotence.

name: testosterone-hydroxylase inducer
description: An agent that stimulates the activity of testosterone hydroxylase. Hydroxylation is the first step in the oxidative degradation of organic compounds.

name: testosterone inducer
description: An agent that stimulates the production and activity of testosterone.

name: testosteronigenic
description: An agent that stimulates production of testosterone.

name: thermogenic
description: Tending to produce heat. The term is commonly applied to drugs which increase heat through metabolic stimulation, or to microorganisms which create heat within organic waste.

name: thymoprotective
description: An agent that acts to alleviate or prevent certain conditions affecting the thymus.

name: thyrostimulant
description: An agent that induces activity of the thyroid gland, in production of hormones regulating growth and development through metabolism.

name: thyrotropic
description: Something exerting or characterized by a direct influence on the secretory activity of the thyroid gland.

name: tonic
description: In physiology, a physiological response which is slow and may be graded. This term is typically used in opposition to a fast response. For instance, tonic muscles are contrasted by the more typical and much faster twitch muscles, while tonic sensory nerve endings are contrasted to the much faster phasic sensory nerve endings.

name: tracheorelaxant
description: An agent that reduces smooth muscle contractility of the trachea that assist in breathing.

name: tranquilizer
description: An agent, or drug, that induces tranquility in an individual.

name: transdermal
description: A route of administration wherein active ingredients are delivered across the skin for systemic distribution. Examples includetransdermal patches used for medicine delivery, and transdermal implants used for medical or aesthetic purposes.

name: trematodicide
description: An agent, or drug, that may be used to treat schistosome infections and infections due to liver fluke.

name: tremorigenic
description: An agent that induces tremors, involuntary muscle contractions and relaxation involving oscillations or twitching movements of one or more body parts.

name: tremorilytic
description: An agent that supresses tremors, involuntary muscle contractions.

name: tremorogenic
description: An agent that induces tremors.

name: trichomonicide
description: An agent that kills Trichomonas organisms, such as in treating vaginitis that is caused by Trichomonas vaginalis.

name: trichomonistat
description: An agent that inhibits growth of protozoan parasites of the genus Trichomonas, such as Trichomonas vaginalis.

name: trichuricide
description: An agent that kills parasitic whipworms of the genus Trichuris.

name: triglycerigenic
description: An agent that stimulates production of triglycerides, a blood lipid that enables the bidirectional transference of adipose fat and blood glucose from the liver.

name: triglycerolytic
description: An agent that promotes the breakdown of tryglycerides.

name: trypanocide
description: An agent that kills trypanosomes, protozoans of the genus Trypanosoma.

name: trypanosomastat
description: An agent that inhibits growth of flagellate protozoan parasites of the genus Trypanosoma.

name: trypsin enhancer
description: An agent that increases the activity of trypsin, a serine protease that hydrolyses proteins in the digestive system of many vertebrates.

name: ulcerogenic
description: An agent that causes production of ulcers.

name: urate-solvent
description: A solvent, such as an organic alkaline solvent, that decomposes uric acid.

name: uricogenic
description: An agent that promotes breakdown of purines in the body, which leads to production of uric acid.

name: uricosuric
description: A sunstance that increases the excretion of uric acid in the urine, thus reducing the concentration of uric acid in blood plasma.

name: urinary acidulant
description: An agent, or nutritional factor, that affects the acidity of urine. Very acidic urine, for instance, may be indicative of formation of kydney stones.

name: urinary antiseptic
description: An agent that prevents growth of microorganisms in urine.

name: urine acidifier
description: A substance that decreases pH of urine.

name: urine deodorant
description: An agent that reduces the odors of urine.

name: varroacide
description: An agent that kills parasitic mites.

name: vasoactive
description: An agent affecting the diameter of blood vessels (and hence blood pressure).

name: vasomotor stimulant
description: An agent that stimulates vasomotor action, as constriction or dilation of blood vessels.

name: vasopressor
description: An agent that causes constriction of blood vessels.

name: vasoprotective
description: An agent that acts to alleviate certain conditions of the blood vessels, such as hemorrhoids and varicose veins. Hormones such as estrogen can have a vasoprotective effect.

name: vasorelaxant
description: An agent htat promotes vasorelaxation, or a reduction in vascular tension.

name: venoprotective
description: An agent that acts to alleviate or prevent certain conditions affecting the veins.

name: venotonic
description: An agent, or drug, that promotes venous drainage.

name: vermicide
description: A substance that is poisonous to worms.

name: verrucolytic
description: An agent that acts against verruca, a contagious and usually painful wart on the sole of the foot.

name: vertiginous
description: Something that causes vertigo, or dizziness, especially because of great height.

name: vibriocide
description: An agent that is destructive to bacteria of the genus Vibrio, especially V. cholerae.

name: viristat
description: An agent that inhibits formation of virus particles.

name: vitamin-A activity
description: Vitamin A funciton, included in growth and development, maintenance of the immune system, and good vision.
